text: {"claims":[{"statement":"The SRMA by Wang et al. found that intakes of >4 eggs/week had no significant effect on blood pressure or blood lipids when compared with ≤4 eggs/week.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"The SRMA by Wang et al. found that intakes of >4 eggs/week had no significant effect on blood pressure or blood lipids when compared with ≤4 eggs/week","entailment":"The quoted sentence directly reports the SRMA result that higher egg intake (>4 eggs/week) did not significantly change blood pressure or blood lipids relative to lower intake (≤4 eggs/week).","population":"older adults","intervention":">4 eggs/week","comparator":"≤4 eggs/week","outcome":"blood pressure, blood lipids","timeframe":"","quantifiers":">4 eggs/week, ≤4 eggs/week","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Significant increases in low-density lipoprotein cholesterol (LDL‑c) of a magnitude of +5.5–8.1 mg/dL were reported in three out of four studies in this SRMA.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Significant increases in low-density lipoprotein cholesterol (LDL-c) of a magnitude of +5.5–8.1 mg/dL were reported in three out of four studies in this SRMA","entailment":"The quoted passage states that three of the four studies included in the SRMA observed LDL‑c rises of +5.5 to 8.1 mg/dL, indicating an effect on this lipid marker.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"LDL‑c increase of +5.5–8.1 mg/dL","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"+5.5–8.1 mg/dL","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Total cholesterol (TC) significantly increased in two studies by +5.6–9.1 mg/dL.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Total cholesterol (TC) significantly increased in two studies by +5.6–9.1 mg/dL","entailment":"The sentence reports that two of the SRMA’s component studies observed a TC rise of +5.6 to 9.1 mg/dL, providing evidence of an effect on total cholesterol.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"TC increase of +5.6–9.1 mg/dL","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"+5.6–9.1 mg/dL","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Since high-density lipoprotein cholesterol (HDL‑c) also rose in two of the studies, this resulted in null findings for the lipid ratios.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Since high-density lipoprotein cholesterol (HDL-c) also rose in two of the studies, this resulted in null findings for the lipid ratios","entailment":"The quoted text explains that the concurrent rise in HDL‑c offset changes in other lipids, leading to no overall difference in lipid ratio outcomes.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"lipid ratios (null findings)","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"The largest SRMA found a positive linear relationship between TC, HDL‑c, TG and LDL‑c/HDL‑c ratio.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"The largest SRMA, which included 66 studies in 3185 participants, conducted a dose‑response analysis for egg intake, finding a positive linear relationship between TC, HDL-c, TG and LDL-c/HDL-c ratio","entailment":"The sentence directly states that the dose‑response analysis identified a positive linear association between egg intake and those lipid measures.","population":"3185 participants across 66 studies","intervention":"egg intake (dose‑response)","comparator":"","outcome":"TC, HDL‑c, TG, LDL‑c/HDL‑c ratio (positive linear relationship)","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"A non‑linear relationship was found for LDL‑c and TC/HDL‑c.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"However, a non-linear relationship was found for LDL-c and TC/HDL-c","entailment":"The quoted passage reports that, unlike the linear associations for other lipids, LDL‑c and the TC/HDL‑c ratio showed a non‑linear pattern with egg intake.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"LDL‑c, TC/HDL‑c (non‑linear relationship)","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"The study found a smaller impact on blood lipids in studies more than 12 weeks long, suggesting adaptation over time.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"This study found a smaller impact on blood lipids in studies more than 12 weeks long, suggesting adaptation over time","entailment":"The sentence indicates that longer‑duration studies (>12 weeks) showed reduced lipid effects, implying a temporal adaptation.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"blood lipids (smaller impact)","timeframe":">12 weeks","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"The SRMA by Rouhani et al. also found a positive linear relationship for HDL‑c, but not for other blood lipids.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"The SRMA by Rouhani et al. also found a positive linear relationship for HDL-c, but not for other blood lipids","entailment":"The quoted text reports that Rouhani’s SRMA identified a linear increase in HDL‑c with egg intake, while other lipid markers did not show such a relationship.","population":"","intervention":"egg intake","comparator":"","outcome":"HDL‑c (positive linear relationship)","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"When compared with no consumption, eating 6 eggs/week was associated with a 4% decreased risk of CVD events or mortality.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"when compared with no consumption, eating 6 eggs/week was associated with a 4% decreased risk of CVD events or mortality","entailment":"The sentence directly states the association between consuming six eggs per week and a 4% lower risk of cardiovascular disease events or death relative to no egg consumption.","population":"","intervention":"6 eggs/week","comparator":"no consumption","outcome":"CVD events or mortality (4% decreased risk)","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"6 eggs/week, 4% decrease","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"A significant 19% increased risk of CVD was reported when the highest and lowest egg consumers were compared.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Another SRMA ... reported a significant 19% increased risk of CVD when the highest and lowest egg consumers were compared","entailment":"The quoted passage indicates that comparing extreme egg consumption groups yielded a 19% higher CVD risk, showing a positive association with higher intake.","population":"","intervention":"highest egg consumption","comparator":"lowest egg consumption","outcome":"CVD risk (19% increase)","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"19% increase","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"For each increment of 4 eggs/week, the overall CVD risk was 6% greater.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"The authors performed a dose‑response analysis finding that, for each increment of 4 eggs/week, the overall CVD risk was 6% greater","entailment":"The sentence reports a dose‑response finding that every additional 4 eggs per week corresponded to a 6% increase in cardiovascular disease risk.","population":"","intervention":"+4 eggs/week","comparator":"","outcome":"CVD risk (6% increase per 4 eggs)","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"+4 eggs/week, 6% increase","modality":"associational"}]}
